LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KDI HOSPITALITY, INC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND/OR STAY OR DISMISS ACTION (CODE CIV. PROC. § 418.10(A)(2))- THE ALTERNATIVE MOTION TO DISMISS FILED BY DEFENDANT KDI HOSPITALITY, INC. (SUED AS DAYS INN) IS GRANTED. THIS CASE IS DISMISSED DUE TO THE ENFORCEABLE MANDATORY FORUM SELECTION CLAUSE IN THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT THAT PROVIDES THAT THE STATE OR FEDERAL COURTS IN THE STATE OF WASHINGTON IS THE "EXCLUSIVE FORUM" FOR THE CLAIMS FILED BY PLAINTIFF NETFORTIS ACQUISITION CO., INC. IN THIS CASE. NO USEFUL PURPOSE IS SERVED BY STAYING THIS CASE. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WIH CRC 3.1312. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss
Off Calendar Jun-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Colby Dean LaVelle II of the LaVella Law Office, Tel. No. 1-510-393-2083 appearing for the Defendant;
No other appearances on the matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KAMAL BHAKTA's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss - DEFENDANT KAMAL BHAKTA's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, To Quash Service Of Summons And To Dismiss - Defendant Kamal Bhakta's motion to set aside default and default judgment is granted. The default entered against Mr. Bhakta on September 25, 2017 and the default judgment entered against him on December 27, 2017 are now vacated. Mr. Bhakta has shown surprise, mistake and excusable neglect to warrant the relief he seeks per the discretionary relief provisions of CCP 473(b). Arguably plaintiff NetFortris Acquisition Co, Inc. should not have proceeded with this case in any fashion after the court's oral ruling dismissing this case based on the mandatory forum selection clause. Granting the motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and NetFortis has not identified any legal prejudice it would suffer by the granting of the motion. Order signed. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

Notice Of Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And To Fix Attorneys Fees Of $8,566.25 (Civ. Code 1717; Rcw 4.84.330)
Off Calendar Jun-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DUYEN T. NGUYEN OF DTN LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-415-310-6979 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KDI HOSPITALITY, INC.'S Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And To Fix Attorneys Fees Of $8,566.25 (Civil Code Section 1717; Rcw 4.84.330)is DENIED. The recent case of DisputeSuite, LLC v. Scoreinc.com (2017) 2 Cal. 5th 968 is on-point authority requiring the denial of this motion. To the extent that this court has invested with any discretion to grant or deny the motion, the court exercises that discretion to deny the motion on the ground that litigation between the parties is still ongoing. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And/Or Stay Or Dismiss Action (Code Civ. Proc. § 418.10(A)(2))
Off Calendar May-03-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. MATT SCHERLING OF BAY AREA ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 510-560-5177 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KDI HOSPITALITY, INC's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And/Or Stay Or Dismiss Action (Code Civ. Proc. § 418.10(A)(2))-
The alternative motion to dismiss filed by defendant KDI Hospitality, Inc. (sued as Days Inn) is granted. This case is dismissed due to the enforceable mandatory forum selection clause in the parties' agreement that provides that the state or federal courts in the state of Washington is the "exclusive forum" for the claims filed by plaintiff NetFortis Acquisition Co., Inc. in this case. No useful purpose is served by staying this case. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply wih CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
